zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Leighton於1476年出生在英國史洛普爾華特萊斯波洛時，他的父親Thomas Leighton24歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Devereux24歲。他於1504年與Matilda Harewell在英國英格蘭史洛普爾皮福德結婚。他去世於1552年2月28日在他的家鄉享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: Leighton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Leighton or Layton. Most, as for example those in Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Lancashire, and Shropshire, are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘settlement’, but Layton in Durham derives from Old English ge-lād ‘road’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English pronunciation of Leighton is usually /lɛ:tən/ or /leitən/ (also spelled Layton and Leaton ), but it can also be heard as /laitən/ or as /li:tən/ (also spelled Leaton ), and occasionally as /lɛtən/ (spelled Letton ).

variants.name: Slayton
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Leighton (compare Layton ) with the addition of initial S-. Alternatively, perhaps a variant of Sladen or of Sleightholme, principally a habitational name from Sleightholme Dale in Kirkbymoorside, but perhaps occasionally from Sleightholme in Bowes or the lost Sleightholme in Lythe. The placenames derive from Old Norse sléttr ‘smooth, level’ + holmr ‘small island, water meadow’. This surname is now very rare in Britain. Compare Slaydon .

variants.name: Laughton
meanings: habitational name from Laughton en le Morthen in Yorkshire, Laughton in Leicestershire or any of the two places called Laughton in Lincolnshire (one near Gainsborough and another near Folkingham). The places are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘enclosure’, meaning ‘village or farm where leeks are grown’. It may sometimes have alternated with Leighton , which is mostly from placenames with the same etymology.

variants.name: Letton
meanings: English: habitational name, most probably from Letton in Norfolk or Latton in Essex. It could also be a variant of Leighton or Laughton . Most of the places so named have the same etymology as Letton and Latton, i.e. ‘farm where leeks are grown’ (Old English lēac-tūn). Another possibility is that it is a variant of Latten .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Leighton was born in 1476, in Wattlesborough, Shropshire, England, his father, Thomas Leighton, was 24 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Devereux, was 24. He married Matilda Harewell in 1504, in Pitchford, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 28 February 1552, in his hometown, at the age of 76.

Il a épousé Matilda Harewell en 1504, à Pitchford, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 28 février 1552, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 76 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Leighton(이)가 1476년 영국 슈럽실르 왓틀레스버루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Leighton(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Devereux(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1504년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 피트ch퍼드에서 Matilda Harewell(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1552년 2월 28일 그의 고향에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Leighton родился в 1476 г. в местности ваттлесбороую, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Leighton было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Devereux, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda Harewell в 1504 г. в местности пичфорд, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 28 февраля 1552 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 76 лет.
